notes: Curcumin. Natural yellow colorant from turmeric. Safe. Has anti-inflammatory properties. Used in mustard and curry products.
Riboflavin (Vitamin B2). Natural yellow-orange colorant. Safe and nutritious. Found in many foods.
Tartrazine. Synthetic yellow dye. May cause hyperactivity in children. Can trigger allergic reactions. Subject to labeling requirements.
Chrysoine - synthetic yellow-orange dye. BANNED in many countries including EU. Should not be in commerce.
Quinoline Yellow. Synthetic dye. May cause hyperactivity in children. Banned in some countries. Avoid if sensitive.
Fast Yellow - azo dye. BANNED in EU and USA. Possible carcinogen. Not permitted.
Yellow 2G - azo dye. BANNED in EU since 2007 for genotoxic risks. Do not use.
Sunset Yellow. Azo dye. May cause hyperactivity in children and allergic reactions. Banned in some countries.
Cochineal (Carmine). Natural red dye from insects. May cause severe allergic reactions. Not suitable for vegetarians.
Orcein - red dye. BANNED in EU. Potential carcinogen.
Carmoisine. Synthetic red dye. May cause hyperactivity in children. Can trigger asthma. Banned in some countries.
Amaranth - red azo dye. BANNED in USA since 1976 (suspected carcinogen). In EU permitted only for caviar. AVOID.
Ponceau 4R. Synthetic red dye. May cause hyperactivity in children. Linked to allergic reactions. Banned in USA and Norway.
Ponceau SX - red dye. BANNED in many countries including EU.
Ponceau 6R - red azo dye. BANNED in EU and USA. Suspected carcinogen.
Erythrosine - red dye containing iodine. Possible thyroid disruptor and suspected carcinogen. Banned in many countries. AVOID.
Allura Red. Synthetic red dye. May cause hyperactivity in children. Possible allergen. Widely used in USA.
Indanthrene Blue - synthetic dye. BANNED in EU. Do not use.
Patent Blue V. Synthetic blue dye. May cause allergic reactions. Banned in several countries including USA and Australia.
Indigotine (Indigo Carmine). Synthetic blue dye. May cause nausea and high blood pressure. Possible allergen.
Brilliant Blue FCF. Synthetic blue dye. Generally safe but may cause allergic reactions in sensitive individuals.
Chlorophylls - natural green colorant extracted from plants. Completely safe.
Copper complexes of chlorophylls - chlorophyll derivatives stabilized with copper. Safe.
Green S/Acid Brilliant Green - synthetic green dye. Approved by EFSA but banned in some countries (Canada, USA, Japan). Possible rare allergic reactions.
Plain caramel. Natural brown colorant. Generally safe. Widely used in soft drinks and sauces.
Ammonia caramel. Brown colorant. May contain traces of ammonia. Generally recognized as safe in small amounts.
Sulfite ammonia caramel. Most widely used caramel. Contains sulfites - may cause reactions in asthmatics. Common in cola drinks.
Brilliant Black BN - black azo dye. Possible allergies. Banned in many countries (USA, Canada, Japan). Should be limited.
Carbon Black 7984 - black dye from coal. BANNED in EU for safety reasons.
Vegetable carbon - black colorant from plant combustion. Controversial, may interfere with medications.
Brown FK - synthetic brown dye. Approved in EU but banned in many other countries. Use with caution.
Brown HT/Chocolate Brown - synthetic brown dye. Approved by EFSA but banned in some countries (USA, Australia). Possible rare allergies.
Carotenes (Beta-carotene). Natural orange colorant and vitamin A precursor. Safe and nutritious. Found in carrots and many vegetables.
Paprika extract. Natural red-orange colorant. Safe. Derived from paprika peppers. Used in cheese and sauces.
Beetroot Red/Betanin - natural red-purple colorant. Very safe.
Anthocyanins. Natural purple-red colorants from fruits. Safe. Found in berries, grapes, and red cabbage.
Calcium carbonate - white colorant, calcium source. Completely safe.
Titanium dioxide - white colorant. EFSA evaluating ban for possible risks (nanoparticles). France already banned since 2020. AVOID.
Iron oxides and hydroxides - yellow/red/black colorants. Natural, very safe.
Aluminium - metallic colorant. Controversial (links to Alzheimer not proven but caution advised).
Silver - used for food decorations. Expensive, inert, non-toxic.
Gold - used for food decorations. Completely inert.
Lithol Rubine BK/Pigment Ruby - synthetic red dye. Only for cheese rind. Limited and specific use.
Common preservative. Generally safe. Used in beverages, cheese, and baked goods.
Sodium sorbate - salt of sorbic acid. Safe like E200.
Potassium sorbate. Widely used preservative. Generally recognized as safe. Found in wine, cheese, and dried meats.
Calcium sorbate - like E200-202. Safe.
Benzoic acid. Natural preservative found in berries. May cause allergic reactions. Not recommended for children.
May cause allergic reactions. Avoid with asthma. Common in soft drinks.
Potassium benzoate - like E211. Possible allergies.
Calcium benzoate - like E211-212. Possible allergies.
Ethyl p-hydroxybenzoate (paraben) - preservative. Possible endocrine disruptor. Controversial, limit use.
Sodium ethyl p-hydroxybenzoate - like E214. Paraben, controversial.
Propyl p-hydroxybenzoate - paraben preservative. Possible endocrine disruptor. BANNED in EU since 2006.
Sodium propyl p-hydroxybenzoate - paraben. BANNED in EU together with E216.
Methyl p-hydroxybenzoate - paraben. Less controversial than E214-217 but use caution.
Sodium methyl p-hydroxybenzoate - like E218.
Sulfites - may cause reactions in asthmatics. Used in wine and dried fruits.
Sodium sulfite. May trigger asthma attacks. Destroys vitamin B1. Common in wine and dried fruits.
Sodium bisulfite - sulfite. Risk of allergies/asthma.
Sodium metabisulfite - common sulfite in wine. Caution for asthmatics and allergics.
Potassium metabisulfite - sulfite used in wine. Caution for asthmatics.
Potassium sulfite - sulfite preservative. Like E220-224, caution for asthmatics. Destroys vitamin B1.
Calcium sulfite - preservative. Caution for asthmatics, may cause severe respiratory reactions.
Calcium hydrogen sulfite - sulfite preservative. Risk of allergies and asthma like other sulfites.
Potassium bisulfite - sulfite preservative used in wine. Caution for asthmatics.
Biphenyl/Diphenyl - preservative for citrus (on peel). HIGHLY TOXIC if ingested. Always wash treated fruit. Banned in some countries.
Orthophenylphenol - fungicide for citrus peels. Possible carcinogen. Wash fruit well. Banned in some countries.
Sodium orthophenylphenate - like E231, fungicide for peels. Always wash treated fruit.
Thiabendazole - systemic fungicide for fruit. Can penetrate peel. Possible neurological effects. Limit exposure.
Nisin - natural bacteriostatin from fermentation. Safe, used in cheeses and preserves.
Natamycin/Pimaricin - natural antifungal. Used only for surface of cheeses and cured meats.
Hexamethylenetetramine - synthetic preservative. Can release formaldehyde (carcinogenic). AVOID. Banned in many countries.
Formaldehyde - preservative. PROVEN CARCINOGEN. BANNED in EU for food use.
Dimethyl dicarbonate - preservative for beverages. Decomposes rapidly into CO2 and methanol (traces).
Potassium nitrite - cured meat preservative. Like E250, can form carcinogenic nitrosamines. Low ADI, limit cured meats.
Sodium nitrite. Used in cured meats. May form carcinogenic compounds at high temperatures. Keep consumption moderate.
Sodium nitrate. Preservative in cured meats. Converted to nitrite in the body. Moderate consumption advised.
Potassium nitrate. Used in cured meats and cheese. May affect blood pressure. Limit intake.
Acetic acid - vinegar. Completely natural and safe.
Potassium acetate - acidity regulator. Salt of vinegar, completely safe.
Sodium acetate/Sodium diacetate - acidity regulator and preservative. Safe, from vinegar.
Calcium acetate - acidity regulator. Safe, also provides calcium.
Ammonium acetate - acidity regulator. Safe in food use.
Lactic acid. Natural acid produced in muscles and by fermentation. Safe. Common in fermented foods and beverages.
Propionic acid - anti-mold preservative. Safe, also produced by the body.
Sodium propionate - like E280. Safe.
Calcium propionate - preservative in bread. Safe, also provides calcium.
Boric acid - preservative. CONTROVERSIAL: possible reproductive toxicity. Limited or banned in many countries. AVOID.
Borax/Sodium tetraborate - preservative. BANNED in EU and many countries for toxicity. Should not be present.
Carbon dioxide - CO2. Completely natural and safe.
Malic acid. Natural acid found in apples. Safe. Used in beverages and candies for tartness.
Fumaric acid. Natural acid found in many plants. Safe. More sour than citric acid. Used in beverages.
Vitamin C. Antioxidant. Safe and beneficial. Found in many foods.
Sodium ascorbate. Salt of vitamin C. Safe antioxidant. Used in cured meats and beverages.
Calcium ascorbate - vitamin C + calcium. Safe and beneficial.
Potassium ascorbate - salt of vitamin C. Safe, antioxidant.
Fatty acid esters of ascorbic acid. Safe antioxidant.
Tocopherol-rich extract (Vitamin E). Natural antioxidant. Safe and nutritious. Protects fats from oxidation.
Alpha-tocopherol - Vitamin E. Essential, powerful antioxidant, safe.
Gamma-tocopherol - natural vitamin E form. Safe antioxidant.
Delta-tocopherol - natural vitamin E. Safe antioxidant.
Propyl gallate. Synthetic antioxidant. May cause allergic reactions in sensitive individuals. Used in fats and oils.
Octyl gallate - synthetic antioxidant. CONTROVERSIAL like E310. Possible allergies. Limit.
Dodecyl gallate - synthetic antioxidant. CONTROVERSIAL, possible allergies and disturbances. Banned in some countries.
Erythorbic acid/Isoascorbic acid - vitamin C isomer. Safe, less vitamin activity but good antioxidant.
Sodium erythorbate - salt of erythorbic acid. Safe, common antioxidant in cured meats.
TBHQ (tert-Butyl hydroquinone) - powerful synthetic antioxidant. CONTROVERSIAL: possible DNA effects, hyperactivity. Banned in Japan, EU restrictions. Limit.
BHA (Butylated hydroxyanisole). Controversial antioxidant. Some studies suggest potential health risks. Banned in some countries.
BHT (Butylated hydroxytoluene). Synthetic antioxidant. Safety debated. May cause allergic reactions. Used in fats and cereals.
Lecithin. Natural emulsifier from soybeans or eggs. Safe and beneficial. Contains choline. Very widely used.
Sodium lactate. Salt of lactic acid. Safe. Used in meat products and cheese. Acts as preservative and humectant.
Potassium lactate - lactic acid salt. Safe, used in sports drinks.
Calcium lactate - provides calcium. Safe, used in supplements and foods.
Ammonium lactate - acidity regulator. NOT PERMITTED IN EU.
Magnesium lactate - provides magnesium. NOT PERMITTED IN EU.
Citric acid. Natural acidifier. Generally safe. Common in beverages.
Sodium citrates. Salts of citric acid. Safe. Used as acidity regulator in beverages and jams.
Potassium citrate - citric acid salt. Safe.
Calcium citrate - calcium source. Safe and beneficial.
Tartaric acid - natural acid from grapes. Safe, used in wine and sweets.
Sodium tartrate - tartaric acid salt. Safe, acidity regulator.
Potassium tartrate/Cream of tartar - tartaric acid salt. Safe, natural baking powder.
Sodium potassium tartrate/Rochelle salt - double salt. Safe, used in pastry.
Phosphoric acid. Acidifier in cola drinks. Excessive consumption may affect calcium absorption. Contributes to tooth enamel erosion.
Magnesium phosphate - acidity regulator, magnesium source. Safe.
Sodium malate - malic acid salt. Safe, acidity regulator.
Potassium malate - malic acid salt. Safe.
Calcium malate - malic acid salt. Safe, calcium source.
Metatartaric acid - tartaric acid derivative. Safe, wine stabilizer.
Calcium tartrate - tartaric acid salt. Safe.
Adipic acid - synthetic acidifier. Safe, used in beverages and baking powders.
Sodium adipate - adipic acid salt. Safe, acidity regulator.
Potassium adipate - adipic acid salt. Safe.
Succinic acid - natural organic acid. Safe, present in the body (Krebs cycle). Used as flavor enhancer.
Sodium fumarate - fumaric acid salt. Possible reactions in sensitive individuals.
Potassium fumarate - fumaric acid salt. Safe.
Calcium fumarate - fumaric acid salt. Safe, calcium source.
1,4-Heptonolactone - acidifier. Safe.
Nicotinic acid/Niacin - Vitamin B3. Essential, very safe. Used as fortifier.
Triammonium citrate - citric acid salt. Safe, acidity regulator.
Ammonium ferric citrate - iron salt. Iron source. Possible reactions in sensitive individuals.
Calcium disodium EDTA - sequestrant (binds metals). Safe, prevents oxidation and rancidity.
Alginic acid. Natural thickener from seaweed. Safe. Used in ice cream and jellies. May inhibit nutrient absorption in large amounts.
Sodium alginate. Natural thickener from seaweed. Safe. Common in ice cream and instant puddings.
Potassium alginate - from seaweed. Safe, natural thickener and gelling agent.
Ammonium alginate - from algae. Safe, gelling agent.
Calcium alginate - from algae. Safe, used in molecular gastronomy (spherification).
Propylene glycol alginate - modified algae. Safe, emulsifier.
Agar-agar - natural gelling agent from red algae. Very safe, high in fiber. Also used as natural laxative.
Carrageenan. Natural thickener from red seaweed. Safety debated - some studies suggest digestive issues. Used in dairy products.
Locust bean gum. Natural thickener from carob tree. Safe. Used in ice cream and baked goods.
Guar gum. Natural thickener from guar beans. Safe in normal amounts. May cause digestive issues in large quantities.
Tragacanth/Gum tragacanth - natural resin from shrubs. Safe, traditional thickener.
Acacia gum (Gum arabic). Natural thickener from acacia trees. Safe. Used in confectionery and beverages.
Xanthan gum. Microbial polysaccharide. Safe. Excellent thickener and stabilizer. Used in gluten-free products.
Karaya gum - natural resin from trees. Safe, thickener. Possible rare allergies.
Tara gum - from seeds. Safe, natural thickener similar to locust bean gum.
Gellan gum - from bacterial fermentation. Safe, powerful gelling agent. Used in plant-based alternatives.
Sorbitol. Sugar alcohol. May cause laxative effect in large amounts. Used in sugar-free products. Generally safe.
Mannitol. Sugar alcohol. May have laxative effect. Used in chewing gum and diabetic products. Generally safe.
Glycerol/glycerin - natural humectant. Completely safe, also produced by the body. Can be from plant or animal source.
Soybean hemicellulose - fiber from soy. Safe, prebiotic.
Polyoxyethylene (8) stearate - synthetic emulsifier. NOT PERMITTED IN EU.
Polyoxyethylene (40) stearate - emulsifier. Safe.
Polysorbate 20/Polyoxyethylene sorbitan monolaurate - synthetic emulsifier. Generally safe, possible rare allergies.
Polysorbate 80/Polyoxyethylene sorbitan monooleate - common emulsifier. Generally safe but recent studies suggest caution with intestinal microbiota. Moderation.
Polysorbate 40 - emulsifier. Used in ice cream. Possible allergies.
Polysorbate 60 - emulsifier. Possible allergies.
Polysorbate 65 - emulsifier. Possible allergies.
Gelatin - protein from animal bones/skin/cartilage (pork/beef). NOT VEGAN. NOT HALAL (if pork). Neutral for health.
Ammonium phosphatides - emulsifier from lecithin. Safe, used in chocolate.
Sucrose acetate isobutyrate - emulsifier in beverages. Safe according to EFSA, banned in some countries.
Glycerol esters of wood resin - emulsifier from pine. Safe, used in citrus beverages.
Diphosphates (sodium/potassium/calcium) - acidity regulators and emulsifiers. Safe BUT excess phosphates problematic for kidneys. Present in many processed foods. LIMIT.
Triphosphates - emulsifiers and stabilizers in cured meats/cheeses. Like E450, limit excess phosphates.
Polyphosphates - powerful emulsifiers. Like E450-451, EXCESS problematic. Common in processed foods. MODERATION.
Beta-cyclodextrin - starch derivative. Safe, used to encapsulate flavors.
Methylcellulose - cellulose derivative. Safe, thickener and natural laxative.
Ethylcellulose - cellulose derivative. Safe, filming agent.
Hydroxypropylcellulose - cellulose derivative. Safe, thickener.
Hydroxypropylmethylcellulose/HPMC - cellulose derivative. Safe, vegetable capsule for supplements.
Ethylmethylcellulose - cellulose derivative. Safe.
Carboxymethylcellulose - cellulose derivative. Safe, common thickener.
Crosslinked sodium carboxymethylcellulose - modified cellulose. Safe.
Enzymatically hydrolysed carboxymethylcellulose - modified cellulose. Safe.
Mono- and diglycerides of fatty acids. Common emulsifier. Generally safe. May be derived from animal or plant sources.
Mono- and diacetyl tartaric acid esters. Emulsifier used in bread. Generally safe. Improves dough strength.
Sodium carbonates. Acidity regulator and raising agent. Safe. Common in baked goods. Also known as baking soda.
Ammonium carbonates. Raising agent for baked goods. Safe - evaporates during baking. Used in cookies and crackers.
Silicon dioxide. Anti-caking agent. Generally safe. Found in powdered foods. Prevents clumping.
Calcium silicate. Anti-caking agent. Safe. Used in powdered and granulated foods.
Glutamic acid. Natural amino acid. Flavor enhancer. Generally safe but may cause MSG sensitivity reactions in some people.
Monosodium glutamate (MSG). Controversial flavor enhancer. May cause headaches and flushing in sensitive individuals. Widely used in Asian cuisine.
Monopotassium glutamate. Flavor enhancer similar to MSG. May cause reactions in sensitive individuals. Used in processed foods.
Calcium glutamate - flavor enhancer. Like E621-622.
Monoammonium glutamate - flavor enhancer. Like MSG.
Magnesium glutamate - flavor enhancer. Like MSG.
Guanylic acid - natural nucleotide. Safe, enhances umami flavor. Often combined with MSG.
Disodium guanylate. Flavor enhancer often combined with MSG. Derived from fish or yeast. Not suitable for those with gout.
Dipotassium guanylate - guanylic acid salt. Safe, enhancer.
Calcium guanylate - flavor enhancer. Safe.
Inosinic acid - natural nucleotide from meat/fish. Safe, umami flavor.
Disodium inosinate. Flavor enhancer often combined with MSG. May trigger gout. Derived from meat or fish.
Dipotassium inosinate - flavor enhancer. Safe.
Calcium inosinate - enhancer. Safe.
Calcium 5'-ribonucleotides - mix E626+E630. Safe, powerful enhancer.
Disodium 5'-ribonucleotides - mix guanylate+inosinate. Widely used, safe. Potentiates MSG.
Beeswax - natural coating for fruit and candies. Safe, natural. NOT VEGAN.
Candelilla wax - plant wax from Mexican plant. Safe, vegan.
Carnauba wax - from Brazilian palm. Safe, hardest natural wax. Glazing agent.
Shellac - resin from insects (Lac). Safe for coatings. NOT VEGAN.
Paraffin/Microcrystalline wax - from refined petroleum. Safe for coatings, not absorbed.
Montanic acid esters - synthetic wax. Safe, glazing agent.
Oxidized polyethylene wax - synthetic wax. Safe, coating.
L-cysteine - natural amino acid. Safe, flour improver. Often from feathers/hair (not vegan) or fermentation (vegan).
Argon - inert noble gas. Safe, used as protective in packaging.
Helium - noble gas. Safe, rarely used in foods.
Nitrogen - inert gas. Safe, used in packaging and whipped cream.
Nitrous oxide - laughing gas. Safe for food use (whipped cream). Do not inhale directly.
Propane - gas. Safe for propellant.
Oxygen - gas. Safe, rarely used.
Hydrogen - gas. Safe.
Acesulfame K. Artificial sweetener. 200 times sweeter than sugar. Generally recognized as safe. Heat-stable.
Aspartame. Artificial sweetener. Controversial. Contains phenylalanine - dangerous for phenylketonuria patients. Avoid if sensitive.
Cyclamate. Artificial sweetener. Banned in USA since 1969. Allowed in EU but controversial. 30-50 times sweeter than sugar.
Saccharin. First artificial sweetener. 300-500 times sweeter than sugar. Safe in moderate amounts. Metallic aftertaste.
Sucralose. Artificial sweetener derived from sugar. 600 times sweeter than sugar. Heat-stable. Generally recognized as safe.
Thaumatin - sweet protein from African fruit. 2000x sweeter than sugar. Very safe, natural.
Neohesperidin DC - sweetener from citrus. 1000x sweeter. Safe, natural.
Aspartame-acesulfame salt - mix E951+E950. Safe (with same aspartame precautions).
Maltitol. Sugar alcohol. Approximately 75-90% as sweet as sugar. May cause laxative effect. Used in sugar-free products.
Lactitol - sugar alcohol from lactose. Safe, prebiotic. Possible laxative effect.
Xylitol. Sugar alcohol from birch trees. Prevents tooth decay. May cause digestive issues in large amounts. Safe for diabetics.
Erythritol - sugar alcohol with zero calories. Very safe, better tolerated than other sugar alcohols. No laxative effect.
Quillaia extract - saponin from South American tree bark. Safe, natural foaming agent in beverages.
Invertase - enzyme from yeast. Safe, hydrolyzes sucrose. Used in artificial honey production.
Lysozyme - antibacterial enzyme from egg white. Safe BUT from eggs (NOT VEGAN). Possible allergies.
Polydextrose - synthetic soluble fiber. Safe, prebiotic. Mild laxative effect.
Polyvinylpyrrolidone/PVP - synthetic polymer. Safe, stabilizer in beverages and pharmaceuticals.
Polyvinylpolypyrrolidone - polymer. Safe, stabilizer in wine and beer.
Triethyl citrate - ester. Safe, solvent and stabilizer.
Glyceryl diacetate/Diacetin - from glycerol. Safe, solvent and humectant.
Glyceryl triacetate/Triacetin - from glycerol. Safe, flavor solvent.
Propylene glycol/Propan-1,2-diol - alcohol. Safe, humectant and solvent. Widely used.
Pectin. Natural gelling agent from fruits. Safe and beneficial. Contains soluble fiber. Used in jams and jellies.
